response: The internet traces its roots to 1960s U.S. Defense Department networking research, with ARPANET’s first UCLA-to-Stanford message sent in 1969 and a decisive switch to TCP/IP protocols in 1983. Tim Berners-Lee’s 1989 invention of the World Wide Web—introducing HTML, URLs, and HTTP—followed by the 1993 Mosaic browser opened the network to the public, igniting a 1990s dot-com boom that burst in 2001 but ultimately produced giants such as Amazon and Google. The mid-2000s rise of social media (Facebook, Twitter, YouTube), the 2006 debut of AWS cloud services, and the iPhone-driven 2007 mobile era further expanded the internet’s reach. Today the web connects more than 5 billion people and underpins commerce, communication, and entertainment worldwide.
